claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Nine WPs in three structural layers: research pillars (WP2/ATU, WP3/BIIS, WP4/CERIA), demonstrators (WP5/NIHS, WP6/ISIA, WP7/ELI), support (WP1/ATU, WP8/FIIT, WP9/BAL)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: work_packages[].wp_id and work_packages[].lead_partner for all nine WPs confirm lead partner assignments exactly as stated; partner_role_matrix[] corroborates.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: 16 confirmed inter-WP data-input edges forming an acyclic dependency graph
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: dependency_map.edges contains exactly 16 edge objects all with edge_type 'data_input'; cycle_detected: false confirms acyclicity.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Nine pillar-to-demonstrator edges: three research pillar WPs (WP2, WP3, WP4) each feeding all three demonstrator WPs (WP5, WP6, WP7)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: dependency_map.edges — nine edges verified: WP2→WP5, WP3→WP5, WP4→WP5, WP2→WP6, WP3→WP6, WP4→WP6, WP2→WP7, WP3→WP7, WP4→WP7.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Each demonstrator WP feeds WP8 (3 edges); WP8 feeds WP9 (1 edge)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: dependency_map.edges — WP5→WP8, WP6→WP8, WP7→WP8 (3 edges) and WP8→WP9 (1 edge) all present.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Critical path runs WP2→WP5→WP8→WP9, representative of nine tied paths of equal length
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: critical_path_nodes: ['WP2','WP5','WP8','WP9']; critical_path_analysis.tied_paths lists nine paths each of path_length_edges: 3; representative_path_selection_rationale confirms WP2→WP5→WP8→WP9 as representative. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json: critical_path field [T2-01,T2-02,T2-03,T5-01,T5-02,T5-03,T8-03] is consistent with WP-level path.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP1 (M1-48) covers governance, QA, ethics compliance including ADRA and GenAI4EU Hub cohesion (SR-09, CC-03/CC-04); D1-01 (M3) and DMP D1-02 (M6)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: WP1 objectives (governance, QA, ethics), T1-03 ('Ethics compliance oversight and coordination with GenAI4EU Hub'), D1-01 due_month:3, D1-02 due_month:6. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json: T1-01/T1-02/T1-03 start_month:1 end_month:48. docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json: SR-09. docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json: CC-03 and CC-04.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP9 (M1-48) manages open-source release D9-02 (M36) and AI-on-demand platform contributions (SR-10/CC-05); dissemination plan D9-01 (M6)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: D9-01 type:'report' due_month:6, D9-02 type:'software' due_month:36. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json: T9-03 start_month:1 end_month:48 — WP9 spans M1-M48. docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json: SR-10 ('Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform'). docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json: CC-05 (same mandate, source_section: call_entry.scope).

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: 48-month timeline governed by 11 milestones (MS1-MS11, M3 through M48), each with a defined verifiable criterion
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json: milestones[] array contains exactly 11 entries — MS1 due_month:3 through MS11 due_month:48; each entry contains a non-null verifiable_criterion string.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Risk register classifies 11 risks
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: risk_register[] array contains exactly 11 entries (RISK-01 through RISK-11).

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-01 (neuro-symbolic integration performance, medium likelihood / high impact) mitigated via modular fallback architecture
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: risk_register[risk_id=RISK-01] likelihood:'medium', impact:'high', mitigation: 'Adopt a modular architecture with well-defined interfaces... Implement fallback modes where the LLM-only planner operates when symbolic integration introduces unacceptable latency.'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-04 (clinical data access delay, medium likelihood / high impact) mitigated via parallel public-dataset strategy and early ethics submission
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: risk_register[risk_id=RISK-04] likelihood:'medium', impact:'high', mitigation: 'Submit ethics applications in M1... Use publicly available clinical datasets (MIMIC-IV, eICU) for initial development and intermediate evaluation.'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-09 (partner commitment / PI departure, low likelihood / high impact) mitigated via deputy PI appointments
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: risk_register[risk_id=RISK-09] likelihood:'low', impact:'high', mitigation: 'Include deputy PI appointments for each WP lead.'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-10 (framework integration failure, medium likelihood / high impact) mitigated via shared API contracts defined M1-M3
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: risk_register[risk_id=RISK-10] likelihood:'medium', impact:'high', mitigation: 'Define shared data representations, API contracts, tool registry schemas, and integration interfaces in M1–M3.'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-07 (LLM landscape change, high likelihood / medium impact) mitigated via model-agnostic interfaces and annual re-evaluation
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: risk_register[risk_id=RISK-07] likelihood:'high', impact:'medium', mitigation: 'Design all MAESTRO components with model-agnostic interfaces... Conduct annual baseline re-evaluation (M12, M24, M36).'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Resource allocation across all nine WPs confirmed by the validated budget response
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json: gate_pass_declaration:'pass'; wp_coverage_results[] shows all nine WPs (WP1-WP9) with present_in_budget:true and inconsistencies:[]; blocking_inconsistencies:[]. Observation: budget_response_reference field reads 'budget_response_placeholder.json' — reference filename indicates a placeholder; this is a metadata concern in the gate assessment artifact itself and does not alter the traceability status of the content claim, which is grounded in the Tier 4 gate_pass_declaration.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: ATU leads WP1 and WP2; remaining seven partners (BIIS, CERIA, NIHS, ISIA, ELI, FIIT, BAL) each lead exactly one WP
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[ATU].wps_as_lead:['WP1','WP2']; all other seven partners have exactly one entry in wps_as_lead. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json: ATU wp_lead:['WP1','WP2']; all other partners wp_lead has a single-element array.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: ATU (DE, HES, coordinator): expertise in neuro-symbolic AI, automated planning, LLM-based reasoning; contributes to WP3, WP4, WP5, WP6, WP8, WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: ATU country:'DE', organisation_type:'HES', coordinator:true, description: 'pioneered work in neuro-symbolic AI, automated planning, and LLM-based reasoning systems'. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[ATU].wps_as_contributor:['WP3','WP4','WP5','WP6','WP8','WP9']; WP6.contributing_partners includes 'ATU' confirming WP6 contribution.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: CERIA (FR, REC) leads WP4: expertise in formal multi-agent coordination protocols and game-theoretic convergence verification; contributes to WP2, WP3, WP6, WP7, WP8
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: CERIA country:'FR', organisation_type:'REC', description: 'formal coordination protocols, distributed AI, and game-theoretic approaches to multi-agent cooperation'. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json: CERIA key_responsibilities includes 'Formal verification of coordination convergence properties'. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[CERIA].wps_as_contributor includes WP2, WP3, WP6, WP7, WP8 (WP1 also present in source but omitted from section claim — listed contributions are all verified).

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: BIIS (NL, HES) leads WP3: expertise in memory-augmented neural networks and retrieval-augmented generation; contributes to WP2, WP4, WP5, WP8
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: BIIS country:'NL', organisation_type:'HES', description: 'memory-augmented neural networks' and 'Pioneers in retrieval-augmented generation'. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[BIIS].wps_as_contributor:['WP1','WP2','WP4','WP5','WP8'] — section lists WP2, WP4, WP5, WP8; all verified (WP1 omitted in section, consistent with pattern across all partners).

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: NIHS (SE, HES) leads WP5: provides de-identified clinical datasets, established clinical validation pathways, and healthcare AI ethics oversight; contributes to WP8
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: NIHS country:'SE', organisation_type:'HES', description: 'Access to de-identified clinical datasets and established clinical validation pathways'. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json: NIHS key_responsibilities includes 'Ethics oversight for healthcare AI application'. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[NIHS].wps_as_contributor:['WP1','WP8'] — section lists WP8; verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: ISIA (IT, HES) leads WP6: operational factory environment, digital twin integration expertise, AI for Manufacturing knowledge; contributes to WP8, WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: ISIA country:'IT', organisation_type:'HES', description: 'digital twins, smart factory systems, and AI-driven process optimisation in operational industrial settings'. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[ISIA].wps_as_contributor:['WP1','WP8','WP9'] — section lists WP8, WP9; both verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: ELI (CH, PRC, associated country per CC-08) leads WP7: real-world logistics operations data across 80+ countries, direct exploitation pathway; contributes to WP8, WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: ELI country:'CH', organisation_type:'PRC', description: 'operating in over 80 countries' and 'real-world logistics operations data and deployment environments'. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json: ELI key_responsibilities includes 'Exploitation pathway for logistics sector adoption'. docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json: CC-08 explicitly lists Switzerland among eligible associated countries. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[ELI].wps_as_contributor:['WP1','WP8','WP9'] — section lists WP8, WP9; both verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: BAL (FI, PRC) leads WP9: LLM engineering expertise, production-grade AI deployment infrastructure, open-source European LLM development; contributes to WP2, WP3, WP5, WP6, WP7, WP8
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: BAL country:'FI', organisation_type:'PRC', description: 'building custom large language models', 'Developer of open-source European LLMs', 'production-grade AI deployment'. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[BAL].wps_as_contributor:['WP1','WP2','WP3','WP5','WP6','WP7','WP8'] — section lists WP2, WP3, WP5, WP6, WP7, WP8; all verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: FIIT (ES, REC) leads WP8: AI evaluation frameworks, benchmarking methodologies, EDIH technology transfer capacity; contributes to WP5, WP6, WP7, WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: FIIT country:'ES', organisation_type:'REC', description: 'AI evaluation frameworks, benchmarking methodologies, and technology transfer through European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs)'. docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: partner_role_matrix[FIIT].wps_as_contributor:['WP1','WP5','WP6','WP7','WP9'] — section lists WP5, WP6, WP7, WP9; all verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Consortium spans seven EU Member States (DE, FR, NL, IT, SE, FI, ES) plus Switzerland (CH, associated country per CC-08)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: partner countries — ATU:DE, CERIA:FR, BIIS:NL, ISIA:IT, NIHS:SE, BAL:FI, FIIT:ES (seven EU Member States) and ELI:CH (Switzerland). docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json: CC-08 constraint_type:'eligibility' explicitly lists Switzerland among the named associated countries.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Consortium combines academic technical excellence, applied research capacity, and private sector deployment expertise across the full value chain from foundational AI research to operational validation
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: organisation_type values — HES partners (ATU, BIIS, ISIA, NIHS) correspond to 'academic technical excellence'; REC partners (CERIA, FIIT) correspond to 'applied research capacity'; PRC partners (ELI, BAL) correspond to 'private sector deployment expertise'. Derivation chain: HES org_type + AI research descriptions → 'academic technical excellence'; REC org_type + evaluation/benchmarking descriptions → 'applied research capacity'; PRC org_type + operational deployment descriptions → 'private sector deployment expertise'. 'Full value chain from foundational AI research to operational validation' derived from the combined TRL-spanning activities described across all eight partner descriptions.
